name: Peking Chicken
recipeIngredient: 2 slices Fresh ginger, peeled and finely minced
2 scallions, quartered lengthwise and cut into 2-inch slivers
1 cup Hoisin sauce or Chinese plum sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 12-inch round or oval microwave-safe baking dish, combine honey, soy sauce, lemon juice, ginger, garlic, paprika, and sesame seeds. Stir well.
2. Coat chicken breasts with marinade, reserving extra sauce; arrange chicken in ring fashion, with ends touching. Cover and refrigerate for 1 to 2 hours.
3. Cover the dish with 4 sections of paper towel, tucking the ends underneath.
4. Cook on HIGH for 6 to 7 minutes, just until chicken loses its pinkness in the thickest part.
5. To heat tortillas, use 3 sections of paper towels. Place tortillas in the center and fold over the sides. Heat one stack for 1 to 2 minutes, just until warm to the touch. Heat remaining stacks as needed.
6. To serve, slice chicken and arrange on a platter. Pass small dishes of sliced scallions, hoisin sauce, cilantro, and warm tortillas.
7. Spread sauce on a tortilla, place chicken, scallions, and cilantro inside, and roll up to eat with fingers.
Makes 4 servings.
